Title: Airbnb

URL Source: https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb

Published Time: 2021-05-14T12:10:53Z

Markdown Content:
From Simple English Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Airbnb, Inc.[![Image 1: Airbnb logo](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/69/Airbnb_Logo_B%C3%A9lo.svg/250px-Airbnb_Logo_B%C3%A9lo.svg.png)](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Airbnb_Logo_B%C3%A9lo.svg)
Company type[Public](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_company "Public company")
[Traded as](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ticker_symbol "Ticker symbol")[NASDAQ](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASDAQ "NASDAQ"):[ABNB](http://www.nasdaq.com/symbol/abnb)
Industry[Lodging](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lodging "Lodging")
Founded August 2008;17 years ago in [San Francisco, California](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco,_California "San Francisco, California")
Founder[Brian Chesky](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Chesky?action=edit&redlink=1 "Brian Chesky (not yet started)")

[Joe Gebbia](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Gebbia?action=edit&redlink=1 "Joe Gebbia (not yet started)")

[Nathan Blecharczyk](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nathan_Blecharczyk?action=edit&redlink=1 "Nathan Blecharczyk (not yet started)")
Headquarters San Francisco, California
Area served Worldwide
Services[Lodging](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lodging "Lodging")

[Hospitality](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hospitality "Hospitality")
Revenue![Image 2: Decrease](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ed/Decrease2.svg/20px-Decrease2.svg.png) $3.378 billion (2020)
[Operating income](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earnings_before_interest_and_taxes "Earnings before interest and taxes")2,553,000,000 United States dollar (2024)[![Image 3: Edit this at Wikidata](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/OOjs_UI_icon_edit-ltr-progressive.svg/20px-OOjs_UI_icon_edit-ltr-progressive.svg.png)](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q63327?uselang=en#P3362 "Edit this on Wikidata")
[Net income](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Net_income "Net income")![Image 4: Decrease](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ed/Decrease2.svg/20px-Decrease2.svg.png) -$4.584 billion (2020)
[Total assets](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asset "Asset")![Image 5: Increase](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/Increase2.svg/20px-Increase2.svg.png) $10.491 billion (2020)
[Total equity](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Equity_(finance) "Equity (finance)")![Image 6: Increase](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/Increase2.svg/20px-Increase2.svg.png) $2.901 billion (2020)
Number of employees 5,597 (2020)
Website[airbnb.com](https://airbnb.com/)
**Footnotes/references**

[[1]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-Company_overview-1)[[2]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-10K-2)

**Airbnb** is an online service for [lodging](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lodging "Lodging"). The name means _Air Bed and Breakfast_.[[3]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-3)[[4]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-4)[[5]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-5) It is available in 190 countries.[[6]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-6)[[7]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-7)

It is based in [San Francisco](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco"), [California](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/California "California").

Airbnb clients have the opportunity to provide housing partially or fully to different travelers, in exchange for a rental fee. The site provides a platform for establishing contact between the owner and the guest. Airbnb is also responsible for [money](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Money "Money") payments. Airbnb offers housing in 65,000 cities in 191 countries of the world.[[8]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-8) From its founding in August 2008 through April 2017, more than 150 million people found housing through the Airbnb website and associated app.

Short-term [rentals](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Renting "Renting") are [regulated](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regulation "Regulation") in many places. Hosts may have to have business [licenses](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/License "License"). They may have to pay [hotel](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hotel "Hotel") taxes. There may be other rules. In [Italy](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Italy "Italy") they should collect 21% of landlords’ rental income as tax. In [New York](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") people cannot rent their homes for less than 30 days unless they are present on the property.[[9]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-9)

Airbnb was founded in 2008 by [Brian Chesky](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Chesky?action=edit&redlink=1 "Brian Chesky (not yet started)"), [Nathan Blecharczyk](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nathan_Blecharczyk?action=edit&redlink=1 "Nathan Blecharczyk (not yet started)") and [Joe Gebbia](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Gebbia?action=edit&redlink=1 "Joe Gebbia (not yet started)").[[10]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-10)[[11]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-11)[[12]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-12)[[13]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-13) The offices were in San Francisco, then in [Hamburg](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hamburg "Hamburg") and [Berlin](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Berlin "Berlin").[[14]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-14)

The company had 21 funding rounds and got $6B in total.[[15]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-15)[[16]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-Austin-16)[[16]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-Austin-16)[[17]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-17)[[18]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-18)[[19]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-19)[[20]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-20)[[21]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-21)[[22]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-22)[[23]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-23)

In 2011, the service won an app award on the [South by Southwest](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_by_Southwest "South by Southwest") conference.[[24]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-24)

In October 2011, the first international office in [London](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/London "London") was opened.[[25]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-25)

In 2012, the company made new locations in [Paris](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [Milan](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milan "Milan"), [Barcelona](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona"), [Copenhagen](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Copenhagen "Copenhagen"), [Moscow](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moscow "Moscow"), [Sydney](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), [Singapore](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Singapore "Singapore") and [São Paulo](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo").[[26]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-26)[[27]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-27)[[28]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-28)[[29]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-29)

In 2015, the office in [Cuba](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuba "Cuba") was launched.[[30]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-30)[[31]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-31)

In 2018, Airbnb Plus and Beyond by Airbnb services were announced to appear.[[32]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-32)[[33]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-33)

In 2020, Airbnb became a [public company](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_company "Public company").[[34]](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_note-34)

29.   [↑](https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb#cite_ref-29 "Jump up")Russell, Jon (2012-11-21).
